exemplar.title: To what extent has the acquisition of Ring home appliances by Amazon would help the company enter into the growing market?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the acquisition of Ring home appliances by Amazon would help the company enter into the growing market?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicit selection and consistent reference to the key concept âCHANGEâ throughout the report
Clear analytical linkage between the concept of change and Amazonâs strategic moves (diversification, market development)
Strong identification of customer focus and ethical considerations as part of core competencies
weaknesses: Analysis not deeply woven through every paragraphâoverreliance on keyword highlighting
Introduction and some sections list rather than define how âCHANGEâ will guide the analysis
Partial integration in some tool applicationsâconcept not seamlessly embedded in every framework
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission clearly demonstrates a strong understanding of the concept of change, with consistent analytical references to Amazonâs strategies. While the integration is strong and aligns with Band 4, it lacks the fully integrated, seamless analysis required for Band 5, hence a score of 4.

strengths: Five supporting documents are cited, meeting the quantity requirement
Sources are relevant to Amazonâs acquisition of Ring and provide headline statistics
Documents deliver sufficient depth to support most arguments
weaknesses: Depth is limitedâmost data points are headline figures without detailed trends or longitudinal analysis
Some citations lack full details or consistent formatting
Limited range of ideas beyond core financial/statistical data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides a relevant set of five documents with sufficient depth to support the investigation, matching Band 3 descriptors. However, the work does not yet reach the in-depth range and richness of Band 4, so it merits a score of 3.

strengths: Appropriate use of the Ansoff Matrix for diversification and market development
Inclusion of STEEPLE elements and core-competency theory demonstrates adequate tool selection
Insightful focus on technological innovation as a core competency
weaknesses: Application of some tools (e.g., STEEPLE) lacks structureâno clear matrix or table
Theoretical frameworks are not fully developed with quantitative or risk assessments
Relevance is adequate but sometimes superficial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and application of tools and theories are adequate and generally relevant, fitting the Band 3 descriptors. To reach Band 4, the work would need a more effective and fully developed application, hence the score of 3.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective integration of sales data (e.g., 1.4 million doorbells) to support strategic claims
Good linkage between data points and conclusions on market entry impact
Inclusion of competitor analysis with market-share figures
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive in places, lacking deeper financial or risk evaluation
Figures and graphs lack axis labels, context on sample size and time frame
Methodology for data analysis is underexplained, limiting analytical rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides some clear analysis linking data to strategic conclusions, satisfying the Band 3 descriptor. However, the analysis is not sufficiently deep or consistently integrated to warrant a higher band, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question with a clear stance on the acquisitionâs impact
Draws on earlier evidence to formulate overarching conclusions
Provides a coherent summary that addresses the investigationâs aim
weaknesses: Conclusions lack specific data points to reinforce claims (e.g., percentage growth figures)
No discussion of study limitations or critical reflection
Some assertions (customer satisfaction) are not directly evidenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although some conclusions are broad and lack detailed evidence, the conclusion explicitly and fully addresses the research question, aligning with Band 3 for answering the question. Therefore the criterion is awarded 3.

strengths: Report follows a clear IB IA structure with coherent headings and table of contents
Logical sequencing of sections enhances readability
Section subtitles (e.g., methodology, analysis) clearly guide the reader
weaknesses: Table of contents page numbers do not exactly match final pagination
Minor pagination inconsistencies between headings and content
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization and structure are appropriate and consistent throughout, fully meeting the Band 2 descriptor for Structure. Minor pagination issues do not detract from overall coherence, thus a score of 2 is justified.

strengths: All required elements are present (research question, word count, figures, bibliography)
Well-formatted title page clearly sets context
Bibliography demonstrates academic integrity
weaknesses: Graphs lack proper labels, captions, and legends
Supporting document citations contain formatting and OCR errors
Dense paragraphs in places reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation includes all required elements, fulfilling the Band 2 descriptor. While there are formatting errors and figureâpresentation issues, these do not compromise the inclusion of mandatory components, hence a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical IB IA structure with title, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, and bibliography.  Headings and a table of contents aid readability, so âappropriate structure throughoutâ is achieved (Band 2).

The sources (industry articles, Strategy Analytics, CNBC, BusinessWire) are clearly relevant to Amazonâs acquisition of Ring and provide some quantitative figures.  Depth is reasonable but limitedâmost data points are headline statistics rather than detailed financials or longitudinal trendsâso the work is ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth with rangeâ, matching Band 3.
The student includes the research question, word count, table of contents, bibliography, figures and citations.  Consequently all required elements appear to be present, satisfying Band 2.
Five supporting documents are cited, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  Therefore the work meets Band 3 for the number of documents strand.

The Ansoff Matrix is applied to diversification and market-development sections, and STEEPLE elements (technological, legal) are touched on.  Application is generally relevant but not always fully developed (e.g., no numeric risk assessment).  This fits Band 3: ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

The student cites figures such as 1.4 million doorbells sold, 33%â40% platform usage and market-share percentages, integrating them into graphs.  While the range of data is modest, it is adequate for the question, meeting Band 3 (âadequate selection and use of dataâ).

Analysis links data to strategic conclusions (e.g., how usage growth evidences successful diversification) but remains descriptive in places and lacks depth on financial impact or risk evaluation.  This demonstrates âsome analysis of the research question,â Band 3.

The research questionââto what extent has the acquisition â¦ helped Amazon enter the growing marketââis explicitly answered in the conclusion, with a clear stance (âto a larger extentâ).  This fulfils Band 3 for answering the research question.

The student employs the Ansoff Matrix and briefly references STEEPLE and core-competency theory.  This constitutes an adequate, though not extensive, selection of recognised business toolsâconsistent with Band 3 (âadequate selectionâ).

The conclusion draws on earlier evidence but does not reference specific data points in detail, and some claims about customer satisfaction are asserted rather than evidenced.  Hence only âsome conclusions are consistent with evidence,â Band 2.

The submission keeps a clear focus on Amazon and Ring.  Background context, market-share data and references to Amazonâs ecosystem show solid analytical linkage between the concept of change and the organisationâs strategic position.  Discussion of diversification, market development and competitor analysis demonstrates that the organisation is consistently referenced rather than mentioned in passing; however, the analysis is not fully integrated into every tool used.  This corresponds to a âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ, Band 4.

The student explicitly selects the key concept of CHANGE and refers to it consistently when describing diversification, market development and core competencies.  Throughout the report the word âCHANGEâ is repeatedly highlighted and linked to specific strategic moves (e.g., entering the smart-home sector, adapting distribution, addressing privacy issues).  Although the analysis is not deeply woven through every paragraph, the concept is applied in several analytical sections rather than merely listed, demonstrating a strongâthough not seamlessâintegration.  This aligns with the descriptor for Band 4: âstrong understanding with partial integration throughout the assessment.â

annotations.comment: Supporting Document 2 entry shows OCR and formatting errors in URL and author fields. Proofread and correct to ensure accuracy.
Well-formatted title with clear course and assessment level enhances readability and sets context effectively.
The table of contents lists â6. Bibliographyâ on page 10, but actual bibliography starts later. Update page numbers to match final pagination.
Clear section heading for the introduction helps orient the reader to the reportâs start point.
Introduction provides Ringâs history, but it does not explicitly tie back to the research question. Add a sentence linking Ringâs origin to Amazonâs acquisition focus on CHANGE.
The paragraph cites Supporting Document 2 but doesnât summarize key findings. Briefly integrate document insights to demonstrate relevance and depth.
The research question wording mixes tenses (âwould helpâ vs âhas helpâ). Rephrase for clarity: e.g., âTo what extent has Amazonâs acquisition of Ring helpedâ¦?â
Listing the key concept âCHANGEâ is helpful, but defining how this concept will guide your analysis would strengthen integration from the outset.
Detailed table of contents provides clear navigation and shows logical sequencing of sections.
Methodology section lists tools but lacks details on how data will be analyzed (e.g., metrics, comparative benchmarks). Specify your analytical procedure.
Clear objective and methodology heading signals structure and supports coherent organization.
Clearly labeled market development heading shows logical progression within Ansoff analysis.
Good linkage of geographic expansion to Amazonâs distribution channels; consider critically evaluating regulatory or cultural challenges in new markets.
The paragraph on consumer behaviour change is dense and breaks mid-sentence. Split into shorter paragraphs and complete the thought for clarity.
Effective focus on technological innovation demonstrates insightful application of a core competency tool.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Appropriate use of the Ansoff Matrix for diversification demonstrates a relevant selection of strategic tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Bold repetition of âCHANGEâ creates emphasis but limiting analysis to keyword usage. Embed the concept into deeper argumentation rather than stylistic highlighting.
Effective integration of sales data (1.4 million video doorbells) supports claims about market entry impact.
Section subtitle clearly segments the Ansoff analysis, improving readability and structure.
Graph reference (âGraph.1â) lacks a caption, numbered label, axis titles, and units. Include these for clarity and proper presentation.
The figure group does not specify axis scales or sample size. Annotate axes and describe data source context to strengthen analysis credibility.
Clarify â2020 Total: 7.9 Millionâ by specifying whether this refers to units sold or revenue.
The 40% usage statistic needs context (time frame, sample size). Explicitly state these details to bolster analytical rigour.
Reference to STEEPLE analysis is made but no structured table is provided. Include a STEEPLE matrix or bullet list to clarify external factors.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Strong identification of customer focus and ethical considerations indicates integrated analysis of core competencies.
Discussion of privacy concerns is well placed, but please include a full citation for the source immediately after the claim.
Competitor analysis incorporates market shares effectively; deepen evaluation by discussing how Google Nest or Vivint might respond strategically.
Graph.2 illustration lacks a title, legend, and axis labels. Enhance the figureâs presentation by adding these elements and a descriptive caption.
Presence of a bibliography demonstrates all required elements are included, supporting academic integrity.
Conclusion section is clearly marked, summarizing the investigation effectively.
The conclusion restates findings but relies on broad statements. Integrate specific data points (e.g., percentage growth) to solidify consistency with evidence.
The conclusion omits acknowledgment of study limitations. Including a brief limitations discussion would enhance credibility and critical reflection.
Citation for Supporting Document 1 lacks consistent formatting (e.g., italics, punctuation). Standardize entries per chosen style guide.
